text: <par>The second syllable <latinform>loŋ</latinform> could be assigned to etymon 3361 <reconstruction>*goŋ</reconstruction> NECK/THROAT on analogy with Sherpa ol-gong (assuming assimilation of initial to the final consonant of first syllable) or to 499 *lom THROAT by analogy with Idu <latinform>e-rõ-lõ</latinform> (35532).</par>
<par>Should there be an /n/ on the end of this word?</par>
<par>1388? m + 208? (208=LOWER BACK). See above  Konyak, Kanauri; also Lotha (p. 7 end)</par>

<par>Pattani <latinform>tsa</latinform> bears a resemblance to <xref ref="1626">#1626</xref>, but the usual Pattani reflex of <reconstruction>*-aŋ</reconstruction> is <latinform>-aŋ</latinform> (e.g. <latinform>hraŋ</latinform> 'horse' < <xref ref="1431">#1431</xref> <reconstruction>*s-raŋ</reconstruction>; <latinform><emph>maŋ</emph>-api</latinform> 'dream' < <xref ref="126">#126</xref> <reconstruction>*maŋ</reconstruction>).</par>
